# HSD 14026867080: Pmax PKG log bits not asserted with IMH1 Pmax events using DAC code or global_pmax_inject

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **HSD ID** | [14026867080](https://hsdes.intel.com/appstore/article-one/#/14026867080) |
| **Status** | complete.broken |
| **Priority** | 2-high |

| **Component** | fw.primecode |
| **Defect Die** | ioe |
| **Conclusion** | fw.bug |

## Classification

| Dimension | Value | Confidence |
|-----------|-------|------------|
| **Root Cause Type** | **FW_PCODE** | 70% |
| **Feature** | Power/RAPL | 52% |
| **Sub-Feature** | PMAX | — |

**Reasoning**: tag contains FIX_PATCH → FW_PCODE

## Root Cause Summary

Sometimes PMAX log bits are not asserted in opc_pkg_therm_status and package_therm_status when an IMH1 PMAX event is generated by modifying the PMAX DAC code or global_pmax_inject on IMH1:

Initial values of PMAX DAC, trigger counters and PKG therm status

In [5]: sv.socket0.imh0.pmax.pmax0.pmax_control.pmax_dac0_hvm_codes

Out[5]: 0x7a

In [6]: sv.socket0.imh1.pmax.pmax0.pmax_control.pmax_dac0_hvm_codes

Out[6]: 0x7c

In [7]: sv.socket0.imh0.pmax.pmax0.pmax_status6.mt0_cbb_trig_count

Out[7]: 0

## Raw HSD Text

<!-- This section provides raw HSD data for agent enrichment (Stage 3b). -->
<!-- The Copilot agent extracts root cause, fix description, code refs, and diagrams. -->

### Forum Notes
﻿[26ww13.3]

Alex reported that Meghana and Kirk are working on a debug patch to improve event handling between IMHs for P Max assertion, with Magana leading the patch development.

﻿[26ww13.1]

The team have some new findings from CTE, Meghana is working on a new patch that has updated IMH-IMH signaling.

[26ww12.4]

* More data/experiments requested by Primecode, Val working on executing this AR today.

﻿[26ww12.3]

Primecode is debugging this on CTE, SIMICS, observed getting assert and deassert fastpath in unexpected orders.

﻿[26ww12.1]

Primcode team has reproduced this issue on SIMCS and continue debugging this over there now.

﻿[26ww11.3]

Alex described how the issue could be reproduced in internal primecode SIMICS by altering the order of asserts and deasserts, allowing for simulation of the same injection flow seen in Python SV.

[26ww10.3]

Patch received yday, patch testing will be done today.

﻿[26ww10.1]

Alex reported that recent data from patch testing differs significantly from previous runs, complicating the analysis and prompting the team to schedule further internal discussions to generate new debugging ideas.

PUNIT Fast Path Investigation: The team observed that the deassertion fast path is being sent from PUNIT while the P Max Semaphore is still set, which is unexpected, and are working with the PUNIT team and architecture experts to clarify the intended behavior.

Patricia confirmed the a new debug patch will be availabe soon and planned experiments to further investigate the issue, while Poulomi noted that additional clarification and experimentation are pending based on questions raised by Mike.

﻿[26ww09.3]

Patricia collected data showing unexpected PMAX event assertions and worked with Primecode team, the PMAX & PUNIT teams to verify if the fast path assertion is expected when the semaphore is set. The team is awaiting further analysis and will update sightings as new information becomes available.
No theory is available yet.
[26ww09.1]


### Description
Sometimes PMAX log bits are not asserted in opc_pkg_therm_status and package_therm_status when an IMH1 PMAX event is generated by modifying the PMAX DAC code or global_pmax_inject on IMH1:

Initial values of PMAX DAC, trigger counters and PKG therm status

In [5]: sv.socket0.imh0.pmax.pmax0.pmax_control.pmax_dac0_hvm_codes

Out[5]: 0x7a

In [6]: sv.socket0.imh1.pmax.pmax0.pmax_control.pmax_dac0_hvm_codes

Out[6]: 0x7c

In [7]: sv.socket0.imh0.pmax.pmax0.pmax_status6.mt0_cbb_trig_count

Out[7]: 0x0

In [8]: sv.socket0.imh1.pmax.pmax0.pmax_status6.mt0_cbb_trig_count

Out[8]: 0x1

In [9]: sv.socket0.imh0.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.opc_pkg_therm_status.pmax_log

Out[9]: 0x0

In [10]: sv.socket0.imh0.punit.throttle.package_therm_status.pmax_log

Out[10]: 0x0

Modifying DAC value to generate a PMAX event on IMH0, trigger counter is increasing and PMAX log bits are set 

In [11]: sv.socket0.imh0.pmax.pmax0.pmax_control.pmax_dac0_hvm_codes = -1

In [12]: sv.socket0.imh0.pmax.pmax0.pmax_status6.mt0_cbb_trig_count

Out[12]: 
0x1

In [13]: sv.socket0.imh0.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.opc_pkg_therm_status.pmax_log

Out[13]: 
0x1

In [14]: sv.socket0.imh0.punit.throttle.package_therm_status.pmax_log

Out[14]: 
0x1

Clearing log bits

In [15]: sv.socket0.imh0.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.opc_pkg_therm_status_log_clear.pmax_log = 1

In [16]: sv.socket0.imh0.punit.throttle.package_therm_status.pmax_log = 0

In [17]: sv.socket0.imh0.pmax.pmax0.pmax_control.pmax_dac0_hvm_codes =  0x7a

In [18]:

In [18]: sv.socket0.imh0.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.opc_pkg_therm_status.pmax_log

Out[18]: 0x0

In [19]: sv.socket0.imh0.punit.throttle.package_therm_status.pmax_log

Out[19]: 0x0

Modifying DAC value to generate a PMAX event on IMH1, trigger counter increases but sometimes PMAX log bits are not set 

In [20]: sv.socket0.imh1.pmax.pmax0.pmax_control.pmax_dac0_hvm_codes = -1

In [21]: sv.socket0.imh1.pmax.pmax0.pmax_status6.mt0_cbb_trig_count

Out[21]: 
0x2

In [22]: sv.socket0.imh0.punit.ptpcfsms.ptpcfsms.o
